messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agents + AHP: Use generated session title for worktree name
ISSUE BODY: <!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Currently, we use the first handful of words for chat session worktree names. It would be great if we instead used the generated chat session title as the worktree name too.

Current: `agents/can-you-please-update-the-vs-code-workbench-4d481cda`
Desired: `agents/update-vscode-chat-session-indicator`

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Instructions  
1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture.
- Invoke `skill: "sessions"`.
2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`.
3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**'
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## `AgentSessionAdapter` — Wrapping Existing Sessions  
Adapts an existing `IAgentSession` from the chat layer into the `ISession` facade:
- Constructs with initial values from the agent session's metadata and timing
- `update(session)` performs a batched observable transaction to update all reactive properties
- Extracts workspace info, changes, description, and GitHub info from session metadata
- Maps `ChatSessionStatus` → `SessionStatus`
- Handles both CLI and Cloud session metadata formats for repository resolution

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### `../../chatSessions/vscode-node/chatHistoryBuilder.ts`  
Converts a persisted `IClaudeCodeSession` into VS Code `ChatResponsePart[]` for replay in the chat UI:
- Reconstructs assistant text, thinking blocks, tool invocations, and tool results into chat response parts
- Matches subagent sessions to their spawning Agent/Task tool_use blocks using `ISubagentSession.parentToolUseId`, injecting the subagent's tool calls inline under the parent tool invocation

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
#### `copilot_chat.session.start`  
Emitted when a new chat session begins (top-level agent invocations only, not subagents).  
| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| `session.id` | Session identifier |
| `gen_ai.request.model` | Initial model |
| `gen_ai.agent.name` | Chat participant name |

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agents + AHP: Use generated session title for worktree name",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agents", "ahp", "ui-improvement", "session-management"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As an AI agent user, I want the worktree name in the Agents view to use the generated chat session title instead of truncated text + identifier, so that I can easily identify which conversation/session I'm working with at a glance.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a new chat session is started via AHP (Agent Host Platform), When viewing the Agents worktree, Then the worktree name displays the full generated chat session title",
    "Given an existing open agent session, When switching to that worktree in Agents view, Then it shows the original chat session title from when the session was created",
    "The worktree name should be truncated if necessary to fit within VS Code's worktree naming constraints (typically 100 characters)",
    "The format should remain consistent: 'agents/[session-title]' matching current pattern but with full title instead of partial text + hash"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate session metadata structure in AgentSessionAdapter to locate chat session title field",
      "Identify where worktree name is constructed for Agents view (likely in agentHost or sessions module)",
      "Modify worktree name generation logic to use session.title instead of current description/truncated approach",
      "Ensure backward compatibility with existing open sessions that may not have full titles"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify the Agents view correctly displays updated worktree names after backend changes",
      "Test title truncation behavior if session title exceeds VS Code limits"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test cases for new chat sessions showing correct titles in Agents view",
      "Verify existing open sessions display their original creation-time titles",
      "Test edge cases: very long titles, special characters, empty titles"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Backend worktree name generation updated and tested",
    "Frontend Agents view displays correct session titles",
    "All existing sessions continue to display correctly",
    "Code reviewed by team member",
    "Unit tests added/updated for session title handling"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Is the chat session title available in the ISession interface or AgentSessionAdapter metadata?",
    "Are there any VS Code worktree naming constraints we need to account for (max length, special characters)?",
    "Should we preserve the 'agents/' prefix in the worktree name, or should it be just the raw title?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The chat session title is stored in session metadata that gets passed through AgentSessionAdapter",
    "Worktree names are generated centrally rather than per-worktree individually",
    "Existing sessions will have titles captured at creation time"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - AgentSessionAdapter documentation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host system-prompt customization",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - chatSessions/vscode-node/chatHistoryBuilder.ts"
  ]
}
```
